title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
After a man with glasses walks away, Mr. Kitcher (referred to variously as Cattermole and Kitcherpool) interacts with Victor Muggleton (also called Mick, McKin, or Mikkelsen), the landlord of the King's Head Inn. Initially, Muggleton asks many questions about London, but his demeanor shifts to cold contempt when Kitcher reveals he is a policeman from Scotland Yard.

Upon being shown to a simply decorated room, Kitcher decides to disclose the reason for his visit to Greyladies to satisfy the landlord's scrutiny: the murders of Lady Siphe (or Brawnshaw), Miss Gransberry, and Mr. Nigges at the Bluxton Hotel in London. Muggleton claims to know the victims but asserts they were neither friends nor enemies, merely people who "got into trouble." Kitcher notes a strange emphasis in Muggleton's words and senses suspicion in the landlord's eyes. Kitcher requests that Muggleton keep his identity a secret, but Muggleton dismissively tells him that no one in Gleghorn will provide valuable information and suggests that if Kitcher wants to ask questions, he should do so in London.

Confused by the sudden change in Muggleton's hospitality, Kitcher goes downstairs to the bar. He observes two inebriated men at a corner table: an elderly man with a white beard and a morose, muscular young man. The young man stumbles out of the inn, after which the elderly man points at Kitcher and insists he sit down. The old man begins singing a song about the "London policeman," revealing that Kitcher's profession is no longer a secret. Kitcher confronts Muggleton about the breach of confidence, but Muggleton denies saying anything.
